description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched, cross-hipped roof with brown shingles and wide overhanging eaves. The exterior walls are clad in horizontal siding with a light beige color, contrasted by dark trim and a dark base. The front facade includes a covered porch with steps leading up to double entry doors, supported by simple columns. Windows are arranged in pairs along the sides, providing balanced natural light to the interior. The structure sits on a concrete foundation slab, giving it a solid, grounded appearance. The overall design is compact and single-storied, typical of suburban residential neighborhoods.
description: A single-story bungalow with a cross-hipped brown shingle roof, light horizontal siding, a covered front porch, and paired side windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, double entry doors, and steps leading up to the entrance.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the paired windows and the extended eaves of the cross-hipped roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain facade, no porch, and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with paired windows and the same wide roof overhang as the other side.
